perturbation.original_text: I. "Confidential Information ". For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall mean all strategic and development plans, financial condition, business plans, co-developer identities, data, business records, customer lists, project records, market reports, employee lists and business manuals, policies and procedures, information relating to processes, technologies or theory and all other information which may be disclosed by one Party or to which the other Party may be provided access by the disclosing Party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of , incidental to or in connection with the Business Purposes, which is not generally available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: I. "Confidential Information ". For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall mean information relating to business plans and financial condition which may be disclosed by one Party to the other Party, which is not generally available to the public.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information', removing key categories like 'customer lists', 'project records', 'employee lists' and information related to 'processes, technologies or theory'. This creates an internal contradiction because later sections still refer to the broader, original scope of 'Confidential Information'. For example, Section II assumes a broader definition when discussing protection and non-disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: II. Non-disclosure Obligations . The Receiving Party promises and agrees to receive and hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Receiving Party further promises and agrees: A. to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure; B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes. C. not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information except as specifically authorized by the Disclosing Party in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement. D. not to use any Confidential Information to unfairly compete or obtain unfair advantage vis-a-vis Disclosing Party in any commercial activity which may be comparable to the commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Business Purposes. E. to restrict access to the Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Business Purposes. F. to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information, and, upon Request of the Disclosing Party, to provide the Disclosing Party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons. G. to comply with any other reasonable security measures requested in writing by the Disclosing Party. H. To refrain from directly contacting or communicating by whatsoever means to the Source(s) of Information without written consent of the Disclosing Party. I. To undertake not to disclose any names and their particulars to third parties without the written consent by the Disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by omitting several categories present in the original text. The contradicted text (Section II) refers to a broader range of information as 'Confidential Information' and imposes non-disclosure obligations based on that broader definition. The narrower definition in the changed text directly contradicts Section II because the Receiving Party is no longer explicitly obligated to protect categories of information (e.g., customer lists, project records, employee lists) that Section II assumes are included in 'Confidential Information'. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition because essential language regarding the scope of confidential information has been removed, creating a contradiction with the obligations stated in Section II.

perturbation.original_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by fax or electronic means with proof of sending machine. The date of receiving the messages, notices or letters is the date of sending authenticated by the sending machine. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above, or upon the facsimile transmission, to the party entitled to such notice at the facsimile number set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by electronic means with proof of sending. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'fax' as an acceptable form of communication, but keeping the sentence that references facsimile transmission, we create an in-text contradiction. Specifically, the phrase "upon the facsimile transmission" is still included in the final sentence, suggesting that fax communication is acceptable, but the initial sentence has omitted fax altogether. This creates uncertainty about the validity of notices sent via fax.
perturbation.contradicted_text: X. Communication : The two parties agree that the communication between the parties is considered delivered and reached other party if transmitted by electronic means with proof of sending. All notices under this Agreement shall be deemed to have been duly given upon the mailing of the notice, postpaid to the addresses listed above, or upon the facsimile transmission, to the party entitled to such notice at the facsimile number set forth below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'fax' as an acceptable form of communication, but the contradicted text, which is the same as the original, includes 'upon the facsimile transmission' in the last sentence. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text implies fax is not an acceptable form of communication, while the contradicted text still references it, causing uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will not, for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will not initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the time frame of 5 years in the Non-Solicitation of Employee's clause, it contradicts the clause regarding the Term and Termination. Where the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information shall continue in full force for at least five years. If there is no restriction in soliciting the employees, yet confidential information is still in effect, then the information can be disclosed by employees who can accept employment with the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited employee solicitation to 5 years. The changed text removes this 5-year limitation. The contradicted text states the Receiving Party's confidentiality obligations continue for at least five years. By removing the time limit on employee solicitation, the Receiving Party can solicit employees who are still bound by confidentiality, creating a contradiction because these employees could disclose confidential information if hired by the Receiving Party, which contradicts the intent of the five-year confidentiality obligation, making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XIX. Attorneys' Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the provisions of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such action shall be entitled to attorneys' fees.
perturbation.changed_text: XIX. Attorneys' Fees . If any action at law or in equity is brought to enforce or interpret the provisions of this Agreement, the prevailing party in such action shall be entitled to attorneys' fees, within maximum amount of $5000.
perturbation.explanation: By changing attorneys' fees to a limited maximum of $5000 it contradicts the clause regarding Remedies. As the Receiving Party agrees to indemnify the other Party against any and all losses, damages, claims, or expenses incurred or suffered by the other Party as a result of the Receiving Party's breach of this Agreement. Limiting the Attorneys fees, limits the ability to collect the indemnification for claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IX. Losses . The Receiving Party agrees to indemnify the other Party against any and all losses, damages, claims, or expenses incurred or suffered by the other Party as a result of the Receiving Party's breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the attorneys' fees to a maximum of $5000. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party agrees to indemnify the other Party against any and all losses, damages, claims, or expenses incurred as a result of the Receiving Party's breach of the agreement. By limiting the attorneys' fees, the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text because the indemnification clause intends to cover "any and all" losses, but the $5000 limit would restrict the ability to fully recover all losses related to attorneys' fees.

perturbation.original_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. The Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect', the modified clause removes the fixed term of confidentiality. It introduces uncertainty and creates an in-text contradiction because the section 'XII. No Solicitation of Employees', indicates 'five (5) years from the date of this Agreement', but there is no time frame for keeping the confidential information from the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will not initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a minimum of five years for confidentiality obligations, which aligned with the five-year term in the 'No Solicitation of Employees' section. The changed text removes the 'at least five years' clause, making the duration of confidentiality indefinite, only until notice. This directly contradicts the 'No Solicitation of Employees' clause which explicitly refers to 'five (5) years from the date of this Agreement', as there is no longer a guaranteed timeframe for maintaining confidentiality.
